title: Phase A · Commit C: Templates cleanup (AGENTS.md rewrite + delete variants/husky/fabric-init three-piece)
description: User-facing template cleanup — most critical commit because these files are emitted into customer projects on `fab init`. (1) `packages/cli/templates/agents-md/AGENTS.md.template`: rewrite all `.fabric/rules/` → `.fabric/knowledge/{type}/`; introduce v2 vocabulary (5 type / 3 maturity / 2 layer). (2) **Delete entire dir** `packages/cli/templates/agents-md/variants/` (`vite.md`, `next.md`, `cocos.md` — v1 framework presets, v2 init-scan auto-detects from forensic.json). (3) **Delete file** `packages/cli/templates/husky/pre-commit` (v1 sync pre-commit gate, v2 model is async-review via `pending/` + fabric-review skill). (4) **Delete entire dirs** for fabric-init three-piece (no v2 successor): `packages/cli/templates/claude-skills/fabric-init/`, `packages/cli/templates/codex-skills/fabric-init/`, `packages/cli/templates/skill-source/fabric-init/`.

files.change: Replace .fabric/rules/ with .fabric/knowledge/{type}/ throughout; introduce v2 vocabulary (5 knowledge types: decisions/pitfalls/guidelines/models/processes; 3 maturity levels; 2 layers: ~/.fabric vs <repo>/.fabric)
Delete entire directory — v1 framework presets are obsolete; v2 init-scan auto-detects framework from forensic.json
Delete entire file — v1 sync pre-commit gate is replaced by v2 async-review model (pending/ + fabric-review skill in rc.3)
Delete entire directory — v1 fabric-init claude-skill has no v2 successor (init pipeline is turnkey, no skill needed)
Delete entire directory — v1 fabric-init codex-skill has no v2 successor
Delete entire directory — source-of-truth dir for the deleted skill assets

implementation: Run `grep -rln '\.fabric/rules\|fabric-init' packages/cli/templates/` to enumerate every template touch point

Rewrite AGENTS.md.template: scan top to bottom, replace .fabric/rules/ → .fabric/knowledge/{type}/; add v2 vocabulary section explaining 5 types (decisions/pitfalls/guidelines/models/processes), 3 maturity levels, 2 layers (~/.fabric global + <repo>/.fabric repo-local)

Delete packages/cli/templates/agents-md/variants/ (recursive — `rm -rf`); update CLI init code if it explicitly references variant filenames (consult TASK-001 init.ts changes — likely already removed)
Delete packages/cli/templates/husky/pre-commit; check if husky/ dir becomes empty and delete dir if so
Delete the three fabric-init template dirs: claude-skills/fabric-init/, codex-skills/fabric-init/, skill-source/fabric-init/ (rm -rf each)
Run `pnpm --filter @fabric/cli build` and confirm template-loader resolution still works (CLI bundles templates at build time)
Eyeball-render AGENTS.md.template through the init pipeline (dry-run init in a tmp dir) to confirm output is well-formed v2 doc

reference.pattern: User-facing artifact rewrite — must be airtight (every customer project sees this)
reference.examples: Follow rc.1 spec for v2 vocabulary; cross-check terminology with planning-context.md Q6

criteria: No matches for `\.fabric/rules` in `packages/cli/templates/` (grep returns empty)
No matches for `fabric-init.*skill` in `packages/cli/templates/` (grep returns empty)
AGENTS.md.template contains v2 vocabulary terms: `decisions`, `pitfalls`, `guidelines`, `models`, `processes`, `pending` (grep all 6 hit at least once)
`pnpm --filter @fabric/cli build` exits 0 with template bundling intact

scope_boundary: OUT OF SCOPE: source-code changes (TASK-001 owns CLI command layer, TASK-002 owns server layer), test updates (TASK-004), schema changes (TASK-006). Template content changes only — do NOT modify CLI init.ts logic that consumes these templates here.

rationale.chosen_approach: Atomic templates-only commit, parallel-safe with TASK-001/002, isolating user-facing artifact rewrite for clean review
alternatives_considered: Bundle template rewrite into CLI commit (TASK-001)
Stage variants/husky deletion separately from AGENTS.md rewrite
decision_factors: Templates are highest-blast-radius (every customer project sees them) — separate commit gives cleanest review surface
fabric-init three-piece deletion is structurally simple (rm -rf), fits cleanly in same commit
Parallel-safety with TASK-001/002 enables real concurrency
rationale.tradeoffs: AGENTS.md.template rewrite is content-heavy (~50-100 lines) and risks divergence from runtime code paths if init.ts (TASK-001) and template (this task) drift — mitigated by TASK-004 integration test gate

manual_checks: Dry-run `node packages/cli/dist/index.js init` in /tmp/test-fabric-init/, eyeball generated AGENTS.md
Confirm generated AGENTS.md has no `.fabric/rules` strings
Confirm generated AGENTS.md has all 5 knowledge types listed
success_metrics: Generated AGENTS.md is human-readable v2 doc
